goal: 恢复 Agent Capsule 分享页 For Agents 右侧入口

summary.title: Restore For Agents share panel
summary.what: Restored the Agent Capsule share page to a two-column layout with a persistent FOR AGENTS / Restore in Codex panel while keeping the Codex-style transcript preview. Updated the worker page test to assert the restored agent entry and absence of the old drawer.
summary.why: The collapsed import drawer made the agent handoff less obvious. The share page needs to remain readable for humans while clearly showing agents how to install capsule, dry-run, and import the complete session into Codex.
summary.user_goal: 恢复 Agent Capsule 分享页 For Agents 右侧入口

decisions.chose: Use a persistent right-side FOR AGENTS panel on desktop and place the same panel before the preview status on narrow screens.
decisions.rationale: This restores the affordance from the previous design without hiding commands behind a disclosure control.
rejected: Keep the compact restore drawer in the header

decisions.chose: Limit the change to HTML/CSS copy and worker test assertions.
decisions.rationale: The manifest, encryption, preview rendering, and command generation behavior already matched the desired import flow.

review_notes: Validated with npm --prefix deploy/cloudflare-worker test, npm --prefix deploy/cloudflare-worker run check, and go test ./internal/capsule ./internal/codex.
Checked desktop and mobile layout in system Chrome via Playwright: panel visible, desktop side-by-side, mobile panel before status, no horizontal overflow, and command text stays within the panel.

architectural_claims: The share page remains a zero-dependency Worker-rendered HTML/CSS/JS page.
behavioral_changes: Share page now shows a persistent FOR AGENTS / Restore in Codex panel instead of a collapsed restore drawer.
Mobile layout surfaces the agent import panel before the preview status instead of hiding it behind details.
Copy button feedback uses English Copy/Copied labels to match the restored panel copy.
security_implications: No change to manifest normalization, preview decryption, bundle download, or import command generation.
Preview text rendering and command population continue to use DOM textContent for dynamic content.
